body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: Transmission of SCHC-compressed packets over IEEE 802.15.4 networks
Transmission of IPv6 Packets over Short-Range OWC

okay thank you very much Lui okay so the next presentation is by myself in this case okay so I'm going to present the last update of the draft entitled transmission of shic compress packets over I e254 networks my coauthor is Anna minauro next please so first of all a reminder of the main goal in this document uh on the left you can see the traditional IPv6 based protocol stack over 15.4 as the read interace uh here six low pan is the adaptation layer between IPv6 and 15.4 and it comprises two main components the first uppermost is header compression and the other one is fragmentation so what we want to do in this document as the main goal is to offer an alternative for the header compression part which would be using shic hea compression uh instead of Copan hea compression and uh Chic is the main product of the lp1 working group and it comprises a heer compression mechanism and the expectation is that it should provide a great performance greater compression degree than six low Pan and the reason is that shik exploits a priority knowledge of the hofi field values of the packets that will be transmitted next please so uh on the status of the draft it was adopted around one year ago today I'm presenting version 05 and there are some updates uh first is in the area of discussing the possibility of rule ID reuse so this means to describe in which cases uh in which conditions it is possible to have disjoint pairs of end points um using different rules however that may have the same rule ID and uh another set of updates is due to uh PR which as an inside joke we call Pro uh remember this is one of the route over modes that we support for multihop communication and um there are several updates uh after a review and several Suggestions by georgeos so thanks a lot for that and also some of these updates were discussed in a Chic working group eim on the 20th of February and then there are also a few other minor updates next please so first of all before going into the actual Updates this is the table of content or the div of the table of contents compared with the previous version and I think that this is the first time that uh there is no changes regarding the structure of the document itself so there are updates uh within some sections but not uh in the structure itself next please so first of all the first update is in section one the introduction we have added some explicit text uh to state that the mechanism to provide the Chic hea compression context to the nodes in a 15.4 network is out of the scope of this document however uh we'll have to monitor the progress of the shik working group work in particular the shik architecture draft perhaps we may have to reference that document and also the same text has been added in section 3.2 which describes the network topologies supported in in this document next please then uh we have some updates in 3.4 which uh describes multihop communication so uh this is in order to provide this uh additional notes on whether the rule ID can be reused so call that uh we have three modes currently in the draft for Route over and then we also have mesh under so for Route over the the first one is the straightforward route over recall that in this case all intermediate nodes all routers store all the Chic rules which are used by all nodes in the network for compression and decompression and uh for this for this reason we state in the document we've added that the r ID must not be reused across this joint pairs of end points because otherwise there would be ambiguity then the second route over mode is the tunnel Ripple based route over TR in this case recall that uh there is a tunnel from The Source up to the root in the network and then if the destination is internal to the six lopan Network there's a downward tunnel as well from the root so here we have added that if all the six alens in the six lopan Network are routing away leaves then a rule ID may be reduced Across The Joint pairs of endpoints because in this case there is no risk of ambiguity because the root can still tell who is the the actual source of that packet because uh it is indicated in the encapsulator address of the IP and IP uh header and otherwise there would be ambiguity so in that that case rule like this must not be reused across this joint pairs of end points so uh next please then the third mode is the pointer based route over uh what we are calling recently Pro recall that in this case the shic compress packet is preceded by a pointer which indicates where in the compressed header is the IPv6 destination address compressed residue and uh therefore this allows any Noe like intermediate nodes to be able to reconstruct the IPv6 destination address of the compressed packet without having to store the Chic rules uh so in this case we've added that in Pro a rule ID may be reused across these joint pairs of end points and uh the in order to identify the different rules used by such disjoint pairs of end points the nodes may use an additional identifier for instance um this identifier might be an IPv6 uh address for instance the IPv6 the sish address or another identifier like the Chik Heather session ID which is being uh developed as a concept in the shik working group Next please so still in Pro we have also modified the figure with the example for this mode in this case basically as you can see we have added that there are two pairs of endpoints for instance AC and DB which both are using rule id2 to identify some rule however perhaps these rules are different but they have the same rule ID again the idea is that there's no risk of ambiguity here and then also there's uh the same with endpoints A and E or BNF which share also the same rule ID for perhaps different rules next please okay so the last one is for mesh under we already had text explaining that it is possible to reuse the rule ID in that case however we didn't have a figure with an example so we have added mostly the same figure as for pro however in this case well the only uh difference is the nodes in the middle which are denoted M correspond to mesh and the forwarders uh recall that in mesh under uh those nodes the intermediate nodes uh are not IP routers so that's why in RFC 6606 we had to come up with this name of mesh and the forwarders which are the nodes called M here in the slide next please so the next set of updates is regarding the pro frame format which has been modified um for several reasons as you will see so the uppermost figure on the slide provides basically what is the 15.4 frame payload then uh the first field the leftmost field is the pro header which we'll see next and then that is followed by the Chic compressed packet and if necessary there's some padding as well well so uh regarding the pro header um here we can see already uh the different fields in this header but now let's describe them and in particular the new ones or updated ones so next please so um the leftmost field in the bra header is the Chic pointer dispatch which was already defined in previous versions of the draft so there are no changes here uh then there are the next four fields which are under the red line which are either new or updated and the first one is the context identifier the CID it is one bit which just signals the prence or not of the optional DCI field which would be uh next so DCI stands for Destination context identifier this is a 4bit optional field which identifies the Prix of the IPv6 destination address so the idea here is to proceed similar to uh the context that is defined in RFC 6282 and this is to allow support of possible several uh different destination address prefixes um because uh this was one of the comments by georgos Otherwise uh there would be only one possible destination prefix to be used and uh some interesting feature is that if in that case if we only want to use a single destination prefix then the additional overhead of these four bits of the DCI will not be present so another point is that we've added that how this context is distributed and maintained is out of the scope so again the context is similar to 6282 meaning that there will be some tables in the notes with some context indicating some short corresponds to some particular destination prefix next please then uh the Third Field is the bit pointer so in the previous versions of the draft you may recall that the bit pointer in Pro uh indicates the starting position of the ip6 destination address residue in the shic compress IPv6 header however here there is some slight modification which is that the now it has to indicate the starting position of the Hop limit field because as prompted by a comment by georgos um the nodes in the middle intermediate nodes xlrs they need to have access to the hop limit field they need to decrement that field when a packet is forwarded so yeah uh we needed some way to expose The Hop limit and um also important to keep in mind as you will see later is that uh we assume that after the Hop limit compression residue there will be the ip6 destination address residue then the last New Field is the bit called HLM which stands for hop limit if it's set to zero it indicates that the Hop limit field has been compressed to its four least significant bits with the four most significant bits being equal to zero which means that we can encode uh up to the value 15 and uh otherwise if it's set to one The Hop limit field is not compressed it's 8 Bits uncompressed uh okay next please and then to support these operation of the pro uh mode then we have to provide some updates also in section 6.1 uh which focuses on compression of IPv6 and UDP headers and actually we need to date RFC 8724 which is the base shic specification which states that in a rule the field descriptors are listed in the order in which the fields appear in the packet header however to support precisely the operation I was mentioning which means that after the Hop limit what we want to find next is the destination address not the source then we have to update UHC 724 to state that in the special case of pro the field descriptors of the IPv6 destination address uh device prefix device IID must appear before the field descriptors of the IPv6 Source address app prefix and app IID so the rest of the fields would appear in the same order as in the IPv6 packet header so um then another addition is that in Pro one rule must be defined for each Direction between the two involved uh endpoints and uh you may recall that uh when shik was developed the context was a an lp1 Network where it is clear that the two end points are a device which means a constrain device a sensor for instance and then some entity called app which is some Network side entity which is perhaps more powerful and in that case a single rule could be defined for the communication in both directions because it was clear who is De and who is up however in uh 15.4 Network maybe that's not so clear and in this case now in Pro uh in order to support this operation now there has to be uh one rule defined for each Direction uh also in addition the destination endpoint has to correspond to the device role next please we have also some other possible update to the base Chic document um RFC 724 again the basic specification states that if the rule is intended to compress packets with different prefix values then match mapping should be used and then regarding the IID part of an address it states that if several iids are possible then the target value contains the list of possible iids the matching operator set to match mapping and the CDA set to mapping sent however uh in Pro a source node must not that's what we state it must not use the match mapping operator of the mapping send CDA to compress the ip6 destination address prefix or IID and that's because uh six lrs do not store shic context therefore they do not know what is the meaning of the match mapping index that would be sent therefore we have to uh say the must note and uh avoid this this Behavior so uh next please is header compression and the other one

hea compression and uh Chic is the main

call that uh we have three modes

Route over the the first one is the
straightforward route over recall that
in this case all intermediate nodes all
routers store all the Chic rules which

this for this reason we state in the

be
ambiguity then the second route over
mode is the tunnel Ripple based route
over TR in this case recall that uh
there is a tunnel from The Source up to

Network there's a downward tunnel as
well from the root so here we have added
that if all the six alens in the six
lopan Network are routing away leaves
then a rule ID may be reduced Across The
Joint pairs of endpoints because in this

because the root can still tell who is

joint pairs of end
points so uh next

Noe like intermediate nodes to be able
